observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accuracy of the reconciling
items reflected in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments in the books
to come up with the adjusted balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account;
b. Municipal Treasurer to request from the Managers of the Municipality’s
depository banks the copies of the debit/credit memos as basis for the
adjustment of the valid identified reconciling items without details in the
books;
c. Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to locate and immediately submit to
the Municipal Accounting Office the disbursement vouchers for proper
recording in the Municipality’s books; and henceforth.
d. strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October
2, 1996.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the inventory of
PPE, indicate whether the items counted are under GF or SEF and prepare a
summary per class of PPE and submit thereon to the Audit Team;
b. instruct the OIC-GSO to update Property Cards for all PPE accounts and the
Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each
category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land to substantiate the
correctness of the general ledger balances for PPE accounts; and
c. require the reconciliation of Accounting and GSO records. Any discrepancies
found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustments should be made
where the error was committed.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the submission of the complete
description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects of
the Municipality;
b. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of the Road
Network System of the Municipality and OIC-GsO to prepare and submit the
required report thereon;
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to maintain subsidiary records
for roads and road components and Local Road Network Property Card to
substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balance for Infrastructure
Assets-Road Networks account;
d. direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the total road network system of the Municipality at year-end; and
henceforth,
e. strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015.

We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and ascertain the validity of
outstanding obligations of the Municipality. Those found valid should be paid
immediately in order to clear the payable accounts. Otherwise, request for an
authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation that have
been outstanding for two years or more, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter,
make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to
Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other
priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to
identify the cause/s of the differences between their respective records and
thereafter. make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.

We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor:
a. file the request for authority to write-off the outstanding cash advances of the
deceased AOs to the COA ATL and/or SA in accordance with Item 8.2 of COA
Circular No. 2016-005;
b. require the Municipal Legal Officer to issue demand letters to accountable
officers who are still employed in the Municipality and require them to settle
their accountabilities to avoid withholding of payment of their salaries or any
money due them, and to those who are already out of government service who
have unsettled obligations to the Municipality to liquidate their cash advances;
and consider filing appropriate charges against erring employees to compel
compliance thereof; and
c. direct the Municipal Accountant to require the AO to liquidate their cash
advances within the prescribed period; and refrain from granting additional
cash advance unless the previous ones had been already settled/liquidated.

We strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Human Resource Management Officer to reconcile the above subject accounts.
and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of
penalties. surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned. Strictly
remit in full the withheld taxes. premium contributions and loan amortizations to
the respective government agencies in accordance with the above-mentioned
regulations.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate
with the OlC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net
differences of P8.357.537.50 and P6.289.589.62 between the recorded balances
of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal
accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required
adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts
in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of
accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the
corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare an adjusting journal entry to record the fund transfer amounting to
P5,754,764.00 in the General Fund books; and
b. conduct thorough analysis of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the
difference in the reciprocal accounts and thereafter, make the necessary
adjusting entry to correct the records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. concerned coordinator/focal person of the projects funded by the fund
transfers from Source Agencies to ensure the immediate and proper
implementation of projects funded by the fund transfers from Source
Agencies;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the liquidation reports of the Fund Transfers
within the prescribed period and to update the records or Subsidiary Ledgers
pertaining to the fund transfers, to facilitate the verification of the outstanding
balance, pursuant to the aforecited regulations; and henceforth.
c. concerned municipal officials to strictly observe the provisions of COA
Circular No. 94-013 in implementing projects funded by Source Agencies so as
to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and that the
services and benefits due to the public are not hampered.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairperson and BAC Secretariat to:
a. furnish the Audit Team copies of perfected contracts and PO together with the
supporting documents within the prescribed period; and henceforth.
b. strictly adhere with the above-cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009-01 for
timely and objective evaluation of contracts and purchase orders by the Audit
Team, so that the Municipality could be immediately notified of any
defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile at the end of each month their bank account records, and effect the necessary adjustment to avoid accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account in their respective records; b. Municipal Treasurer to request from the Managers of the Municipality’s depository banks the copies of the debit/credit memos as basis for the adjustment of the valid identified reconciling items without details in the books; and d. Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to locate and immediately submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the disbursement vouchers for proper recording in the Municipality’s books. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports/documents shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of her salaries until she shall have complied therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against her, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to use the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible form each of them as provided by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer at the start of the year to serve as basis in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a thorough analysis of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts due to the inclusion of advance payment in the set-up of the said Receivable accounts and prepare an adjusting entry in order to reflect the correct balances of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts as at year-end; and

c. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts in order to reflect in the correct balance in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor to file the request for authority to write-off the outstanding cash advances of deceased AOs to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Moreover, we strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Legal Officer to issue demand letters to accountable officers who are still employed in the Municipality and require them to settle their accountabilities to avoid withholding of payment of their salaries or any money due them, and to those who are already out of government service who have unsettled obligations to the Municipality to liquidate their cash advances; and consider filing appropriate charges against erring employees to compel compliance thereof;

b. Municipal Accountant to require the AOs to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period; and

c. Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advance unless the previous ones are already liquidated or settled.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and ascertain the validity of outstanding obligations of the Municipality. Those found valid should be paid immediately in order to clear the payable accounts. Otherwise, request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation that have been outstanding for two years or more, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts and investigate/determine the causes of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter prepare the required adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to identify the cause of the discrepancies between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the OIC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the discrepancies of P(8,327,712.70) and P(7,299,759.66) between the recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently prepare the required adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough analysis of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the difference in the reciprocal accounts; and thereafter. make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the above subject accounts. and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties. surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes. premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations; and

b. Municipal Accountant to expedite and prioritize the determination of the composition of the unaccounted and abnormal beginning balances amounting to P3.113.399.78 and P(331.905.48). respectively. in coordination with the other concerned government agencies so that appropriate disposition on said balances can be undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the Bids and Awards Committee to send written invitations to the representative of the COA and at least two other observers; ensure that such invitations are received at least five calendar days before the date of the procurement stage/activity to enhance the transparency of the process; and

b. instruct the BAC Secretariat through the BAC Chairman to strictly comply with the posting requirement in the PhilGEPS website of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, infrastructure projects and services as required under Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to improve transparency in the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to submit within the prescribed period all their accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the abovementioned regulations, to facilitate timely review/post-audit of the financial transactions of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to strictly adhere with the above cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009-01 for timely and objective evaluation of the same by the Audit Team, so that the Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We strongly reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of all the Municipality’s
PPE and prepare and submit the required report thereon. Any discrepancies between the
inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and
make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed;
b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to locate the related documents on damaged
properties razed by fire as guide for Management on what proper action to be
undertaken to drop the cost of the same from the books of accounts of the Municipality;
c. General Services Officer and the Property Officer to make certain that Property
Cards and AREs are well maintained, complete and updated to facilitate the physical
count of property and equipment; and
d. Municipal Accountant to continue exerting efforts to trace back and
substantiate the old balances of the PPE accounts in order to provide the correct
depreciation for each type of properties of the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct
the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile at the end of
each month their bank account records, and effect the necessary adjustment to avoid
accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in their
respective records;
b. Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accuracy of the reconciling
items reflected in the bank reconciliation Statements and effect the necessary
adjustments in the books to come up with the adjusted balance of the Cash in Bank account ;
c. Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the depository banks and
formally request for copies of the Debit/Credit Memos to support adjustment in the
books; and
d. Municipal Treasurer to immediately locate the Disbursement Vouchers for the
necessary recording in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of
taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of them at the
start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special
Education Tax Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS
for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to come up
with the details of the accounts showing the complete list of the taxpayers and the
amounts collectible from them in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivable
accounts balances.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. continuously evaluate and verify the balances comprising the Accounts
Payable account and cause the reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus of those balances
which are two years old or over and/or other undocumented Accounts Payable
irrespective of age, pursuant to Commission on Audit Circular No. 76-45 and Executive
Order No. 228, as implemented by Letter of Instruction No. 478 and Section 98 of
Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Sec 157 of GAAM Volume I , for the fair
presentation of the account balance in the financial statements; and
b. determine the noted discrepancy of (P149,760,175.53) between the balance of
Accounts Payable per financial statements and schedule of Accounts Payable account to
provide a reliable Schedule of Accounts Payable .

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary supporting
documents to substantiate the Municipality’s request for write-off in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
b. request for a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution approving or concurring the
request for the write-off of the account.
Moreover, we strongly reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that
Management:
a. thru its Legal Office, issue demand letters to accountable officers who are still
employed in the Municipality and require them to settle their accountabilities
to avoid withholding of payment of their salaries or any money due them, and
to those who are already out of government service who have unsettled
obligations to the Municipality to liquidate their cash advances;
b. consider filing appropriate charges against erring employees to compel
compliance thereof; and
c. perform all available remedies under COA Circular No. 97-002 to enforce
settlement of the unliquidated cash advances;
d. refrain from granting additional cash advance unless the previous ones are already
liquidated or settled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to
LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts
for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the
implementation of the above-cited projects to immediately address the issue
that may arise during its implementation and to avoid additional delays in the
completion of the same; and
b. require the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to properly identify and prioritize
the projects to be submitted to DILG for funding under LGSF-AM in the future to
ensure the timely implementation of the said projects for the benefits of the general
public.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require
the concerned officials to prepare the required report on time and provide the assigned
Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader with a list of all ongoing PPAs, and those
that are to be implemented during the year, for audit validation and verification. Also,
strictly comply with the provisions of the afore-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to prepare and
submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the
prescribed period in accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of R.A. No.
9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. 0IC, GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network property and equipment.
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the OIC, GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and thereafter,
c. Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.

The Municipality’s Municipal Health Officer made sure that all drugs purchased are included and listed in PNDF Volume 1, latest edition. The concerned Municipal officials thru the Office of the Municipal Legal Officer submitted explanation together with the lacking documents in compliance with the issued Notice of Suspensions, for evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The concerned Municipal officials submitted explanation together with the lacking documents in compliance with the issued Notice of Suspensions, for evaluation with the issued Notices of Suspensions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund to transfer and replace the amount of P2,158,294.67 charged to the Special Education Fund instead of General Fund. Thereafter, the Municipality should strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and DECS/DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, 01-A, 01-B and 04 in the utilization of SEF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer appropriated fund in the GF to transfer the amount of P2,158,294.67 to the SEF. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant set up fund transfer of the said amount from GF to SEF per JEV Nos. 200-1612-1188 and 1189 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit immediately to the Auditor the CRAAF for the months of September 2011 to December 2015 for verification. Likewise, strictly comply with the requirements of Section 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, to ensure that all accountable forms received and issued are properly accounted for and to prevent fraudulent use of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the CRAAF for the months of September 2011 to December 2015 (except September 2014) to the Auditor, and is now practicing the monthly submission of the CRAAF to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the contractors to refund the overpayment of P4,101,721.62 to the Municipality. Otherwise, the municipal officials concerned should be held liable to the deficiencies noted in audit.
observations.managementAction: The concerned contractors refunded to the Municipality the amount of P2,816,913.51 relative to the noted overpayment due to inclusion of ESAO. Out of the P4,101,721.62 overpayment, only P2,816,913.51 were refunded by the concerned contractors, thus leaving a balance of P1,284,808.11 which will be subject for issuance of Notice of Disallowance by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Out of the P4,101,721.62 overpayment subject for refund, only P2,816,913.51 were refunded by the concerned contractors, thus leaving a balance of P1,284,808.11 which will be subject for issuance of Notice of Disallowance by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the contractors to refund the total amount of P464,129.30 for the deficiency found in the implementation of the above-mentioned projects. Otherwise, the concerned Municipal officials should be held accountable thereon; the Municipal Engineer to monitor regularly the implementation of all on-going infrastructure projects of the Municipality to ensure that these are implemented in accordance with the plans and specifications being required by the Management.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive submitted a written comments and explanation relative to the deficiencies noted in the implementation of the three (3) infrastructure projects. Results of COA re-inspection conducted on September 13, 2016 disclosed that deficiencies noted were already rectified or corrected and management’s comments on the said three (3) infrastructure projects can now be given due consideration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials/employees of the Municipality’s Engineering Department prepare the ABC and cost estimates for infrastructure projects based on established technical standards to ensure reasonableness of the contract cost. Also, the Bids and Awards Committee should ensure that the ABC of the projects to be bidded and the contract amounts of the projects to be awarded do not include the cost of ESAO.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials/employees of the Municipality’s Engineering Department make sure that the ABC and cost estimates for infrastructure projects were based on established technical standards. Also, the BAC is now making sure that cost of EAO is not included in the ABC of the projects to be bidded and the contract amounts of the projects to be awarded. The contractors concerned refunded the total amount of P686,856.15 relative to the excessive cost of the projects. Out of the P2,167,406.08 excess cost of the 13 infrastructure projects, only P686,856.15 were refunded by the concerned contractors, thus leaving a balance of P1,480,549.93 which will be subject for issuance of Notice of Disallowance by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some of the Disbursement Vouchers for the procurement of infrastructure projects were not supported with the ABC. Out of the P2,167,406.08 excess cost of the 13 infrastructure projects, only P686,856.15 were refunded by the concerned contractors, thus leaving a balance of P1,480,549.93 which will be subject for issuance of Notice of Disallowance by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), direct the GAD Technical Working Group (TWG) or secretariat which is composed of various offices within the Municipality to actively participate in the development and maintenance of a GAD Database containing gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data that should have been systematically gathered, regularly updated and subjected to gender analysis for planning, programming, and policy formulation in compliance with Section 37 D of MCW-IRR, to ensure that GAD programs, activities and projects included in the GAD Plan and Budget would answer to gender issues and concerns occurring in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality’s GAD TWG is now maintaining its GAD Database as source of information for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund – LDRRMF to transfer and replace the amount charged to Trust Liability-DRRM in the amount of P12,886,485.75 which should be charged to the General Fund; and thereafter, the Municipal Accountant should prepare necessary correcting entries to revert the amount of P12,886,485.75 from General Fund to Trust Fund, and to reclassify the entry made in recording the purchase of dump truck costing P1,794,000.00, from “Disaster and Rescue Equipment to “Construction and Heavy Equipment” in accordance with the accounting and reporting guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer appropriated fund of P10,104,300 in the GF-LDRRMF to transfer and replace the amount charged to Trust Liability-DRRM, then the Municipal Accountant prepared correcting entries to revert the amount of P12,886,485.75 per JEV Nos. 300-1606-0401-A dated 6/24/2016 and 300-1612-0852 dated 12/29/2016, respectively. Moreover, reclassification on entry was made by the Municipal Accountant for the recording of purchased dump truck per JEV No. 100-16-12-8073 dated 12/29/16.
observations.reason: The appropriation made by the Municipal Budget Officer in the GF-LDRRMF for transfer to Trust Liability-DRRM is short by P2,782,185.75.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to prepare and submit the necessary reports promptly and regularly to the Audit Team as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Moreover, ensure that the 5% allocation for LDRRM Fund is fully utilized based on the approved LDRRMFIP for CY 2016. Provide for specific targets and timelines for such programs/projects/ activities to clearly establish the progress and success of implementation.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO complied with the submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund but still failed to submit to the Audit Team the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2016.
